config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I believe society matters, but individual rights should not be treated as secondary by default, because once the state or majority can override them too easily, abuse becomes much more likely. From my perspective, a society is healthier when it protects personal freedom, dignity, and due process, while only limiting rights in narrow cases for real public harm. In practice, prioritizing society too broadly often ends up silencing minorities and justifying unfair treatment.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis didahulukan di atas hak individu. Dari perspektif saya sebagai orang Amerika, pengalaman politik kami menunjukkan bahwa ketika “demi masyarakat” dipakai terlalu longgar, itu sering menjadi alasan untuk membatasi kebebasan berbicara, privasi, dan perlakuan yang adil, terutama bagi kelompok minoritas. Hak individu memang tidak mutlak tanpa batas, tetapi pembatasannya harus sangat sempit, jelas, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan, bukan sekadar karena mayoritas merasa itu lebih nyaman. Masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun dengan melindungi hak orang per orang, karena di situlah batas terhadap penyalahgunaan kekuasaan benar-benar diuji.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should automatically take priority over individual rights. From an Indonesian perspective, I have seen how arguments about public order, harmony, or the common good can be used to pressure people into silence, and that makes me wary of giving society a blanket higher status. At the same time, individual rights are not an excuse to ignore real harms to others, so limits can be justified for clear public safety or serious social damage, but they must be narrow and accountable. For me, a fair society protects people first, because once rights become easy to override, the strongest voices usually win and the rest are left behind.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu karena hak individu tidak boleh dianggap nomor dua secara default. Sebagai orang Amerika, saya melihat betapa mudahnya alasan “demi masyarakat” dipakai untuk membenarkan sensor, pelanggaran privasi, atau perlakuan tidak adil terhadap minoritas, dan itu menurut saya bahaya yang nyata. Kepentingan publik memang penting, tetapi pembatasan hak harus sangat sempit, jelas, dan bisa diuji, bukan sekadar karena mayoritas merasa lebih nyaman. Masyarakat yang kuat justru lahir dari perlindungan hak orang per orang, bukan dari kebiasaan mengorbankan individu demi slogan kolektif.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe the interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From my Indonesian perspective, I understand the appeal of harmony, order, and the common good, but I also know how easily those words can be used to pressure people into silence or to excuse unfair treatment of minorities. Society does matter, but it should be built by protecting individual rights first, with limits only in narrow cases where there is clear harm, not just because the majority wants it. If we make the collective automatically superior, the people with the least power are usually the first to lose.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis didahulukan di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman Amerika, alasan “demi kebaikan bersama” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan sensor, pelanggaran privasi, dan perlakuan tidak adil terhadap minoritas, jadi saya tidak percaya pada prioritas kolektif yang terlalu luas. Masyarakat memang punya kepentingan nyata, tetapi pembatasan hak harus sempit, jelas, dan benar-benar bisa dipertanggungjawabkan. Kalau tidak, yang terjadi bukan ketertiban yang sehat, melainkan kekuasaan mayoritas yang mudah menyalahgunakan orang yang paling lemah.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
